paragraphs.text:
Nodes (species) involved
| SRD‑46 shorthand | Species (interpretation) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| [M] | Zn2+ | Free zinc(II) aquo ion |
| [L] | deprotonated histidine (His−) | Imidazole donor deprotonated overall; anionic ligand |
| [HL] | neutral histidine (zwitterion) | Protonated form that can bind through imidazole N |
| [ML] | Zn(His) | 1:1 Zn–histidinate complex |
| [ML2] | Zn(His)2 | 1:2 bis‑histidinate complex |
| [MHL] | Zn(His)(HHis) | 1:1 complex with one neutral HL (protonated adduct) |
| [MHL2] | Zn(HHis)2 | 1:2 complex with two neutral HL ligands |
| [M(HL)2] | Zn(HHis)2 (isomeric/proton‑linked form) | Proton‑linked form reached via intracomplex protonation (see edges) |
| [M(OH)L2] | Zn(OH)(His)2 | Hydroxo‑substituted bis complex (appears at higher basicity) |
| [H] | H+ | Proton node coupling acid–base and coordination steps |
| [H2O] | H2O | Water node (balances hydroxo/protonation steps) |

|---|---|

grounded_claims.evidence_snippet: Filler span: no useful database-groundable information.

paragraphs.text:
Chemistry notes
- Histidine binds Zn(II) primarily through the imidazole nitrogen; as the ligand deprotonates (L−), anionic histidinate favors [ML] and [ML2]. Neutral HL can form protonated adducts ([MHL], [MHL2]).  
- Under more basic conditions, hydroxo substitution appears, giving [M(OH)L2]; protonation reversibly converts it back to [ML2], linking metal–hydroxo chemistry with ligand binding.  
- This set of nodes captures stepwise complexation, proton-linked speciation, and hydrolysis within a single Zn–histidine network.
